strengths: PostgreSQL and cloud data tools knowledge
weaknesses: No Airflow or workflow orchestration evidence
Fewer than 5 years in titled analytics engineer roles
No mention of Hex for dashboards
Limited explicit startup analytics leadership experience
improvement_bullets: Developed robust data models and automated multi-step ETL pipelines using SQL and Python, ensuring high data integrity and establishing SLAs for critical business reporting.
Built and maintained scalable data pipelines using dbt and Git to transform raw data into canonical datasets for cross-functional GTM and Product stakeholders.
Engineered end-to-end analytics workflows by integrating Python scripts with orchestration tools (similar to Airflow) to ensure reliable, high-uptime delivery of self-serve datasets.
Leveraged a full-stack mindset to build interactive dashboards in Hex and Power BI, enabling autonomous, self-serve data discovery across company departments.
Adopted a bias for action in a startup-paced environment, proactively identifying data architecture gaps and implementing scalable solutions to support rapid organizational growth.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Analytics Data Engineer opportunity with Anthropic. I am interested in this opportunity because of Anthropic's commitment to creating reliable and steerable AI systems that prioritize safety and human benefit. What interests me about working for Anthropic is its unique approach to treating AI research as an empirical science, which inspires me because it mirrors my own data-driven methodology of turning complex information into actionable clarity. I believe my expertise in end-to-end data pipeline development and my proficiency in building scalable analytics models would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a full-stack mindset, focusing on the entire data lifecycle. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a gap in operational visibility and developed automated workflows and interactive dashboards that reduced reporting time by 40%. By translating complex performance data into clear, self-serve insights, I empowered the team to increase new customer acquisition by 300% over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Analytics Data Engineer role at Anthropic would allow me to bring my technical rigor in SQL, Python, and dbt to the mission of building canonical datasets that support company-wide decision-making. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my ability to thrive in ambiguity. When tasked with streamlining client onboarding, I took the initiative to architect a new data-driven workflow from scratch, integrating multiple tools to automate manual entry. This improved team efficiency by 25% and ensured consistent service delivery during a period of rapid scaling. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in cloud-based data transformation in working on foundational data products at Anthropic. I know that my technical versatility and commitment to data integrity align with what it takes to succeed in the Analytics Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
